key_insight: A unified, elementary proof gives both mean-square and sub-Gaussian concentration bounds for contractive stochastic approximation with multiplicative noise and potentially unbounded iterates, without smoothing the norm.
engineering_relevance: This strengthens reliability guarantees for reinforcement-learning and other iterative control/optimization methods where updates are contractive but noise depends on the state size.
method: Use an averaged-noise sequence and auxiliary iterates to derive a one-step Lyapunov drift inequality directly on the normed error.
Prove boundedness in expectation via induction to obtain the mean-square bound.
For concentration, run a probabilistic induction on “good” events so Azuma-Hoeffding can be applied over controlled trajectories.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical guarantees only: mean-square bounds and the first sub-Gaussian-tailed maximal all-time concentration bound for SA under multiplicative noise, with no empirical evaluation mentioned.
watch_item: The concentration result depends on a stepsize that scales logarithmically with the confidence level, so practical tuning may be more delicate under tight risk constraints.
paper_title: Concentration and Mean-Square Bounds for Contractive Stochastic Approximation: A Unified Elementary Approach
